# Shared Lessons

- Keep answers grounded in the repo, instructions, MCP tools, and the web.
- Prefer adapted public prompts over writing a new prompt from scratch.
- When a user wants open-source-style workflow files, adapt the section structure and output contract from the public pattern, but keep the actual wording repo-specific and original.
- Strong prompts usually use clear sections, delimiters, explicit output format, and examples only when they reduce ambiguity.
- Keep the workflow small enough that it is easy to re-read before responding.
- When integrating a user-provided workflow bundle, copy compatible files verbatim first, then reconcile only the overlapping local files.
- After a bulk import, validate frontmatter, `applyTo` patterns, and nested reference paths before treating the setup as complete.
- Record repeated mistakes, user wishes, useful fixes, and decisions here.
- When this file gets too long, condense it and remove low-value detail.
